recent_calls.summary: Leslie called to report that her cooling system is running but not blowing cold air. She requested the earliest possible service appointment for the next day, which was noted by the agent. Leslie declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the quick and responsive service.

The user, Dustin Orf, called to schedule an appointment for a main sewer line blockage with a preferred service window between 8 AM and 4 PM the next day. The agent collected all necessary details including the service address and contact number, and the user declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests. The agent confirmed the request and informed Dustin that the team would follow up to confirm availability.
The call was very brief with the user only greeting the agent before hanging up. No further conversation or assistance took place.

Richard called to report that his air conditioning is not working again after recent service and requested a technician to come out the next business day, preferably tomorrow as soon as possible, with a blower motor. The agent confirmed the service details and contact information, and the call concluded without further questions or feedback from Richard.

Darren Neil called to confirm details for a required heating system inspection at his property in Saint Louis County. The agent collected all necessary information, including the preferred Saturday late morning or afternoon time slot, and informed Darren that the team would contact him to confirm the appointment. Darren declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.
The call was a brief inbound conversation where the agent introduced herself as Budget's after-hours assistant, but the user responded with a different name and the call ended shortly after.

The user called about an issue with their thermostat showing 18.5 and wanted assistance with their AC. The agent confirmed it was not an emergency and began collecting user information for a follow-up service. The call ended before the agent could complete the information gathering.

Vivian Li called to request a technician to inspect and provide a quote for replacing her old air conditioner. She provided her service address, contact details, and preferred appointment time. The agent noted her interest in home maintenance programs but Vivian later declined the information.

The user, Tasha, called after hours seeking help with her air conditioner. The agent informed her that live representatives were unavailable and assured her that someone would call her back on the next business day. The call ended with no further assistance provided during the call.

The user called to request an earlier appointment time for a service scheduled at 1517 College Avenue. The agent confirmed the address and informed the user that the scheduling team would contact her on the next business day to confirm availability. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and no further requests.

The user, Firia Amsad, scheduled a plumbing inspection for a water heater installed in 2022 that has not yet been inspected. The preferred appointment time is Wednesday the 22nd at 1 PM or in the afternoon. The user declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide an email for follow-up.

The user called to report that their air conditioner stopped working and requested a technician to call them for service. The agent collected the user's contact information and service address, confirmed the issue, and assured the user that the team would call them to discuss the service. The user declined to provide an email and preferred a callback rather than scheduling an immediate appointment.

The user, Camden, called after hours requesting emergency services. The agent, Evie, informed the user that a live representative was not available but documented the request for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the agent offering further assistance.

The user called after hours wanting to speak to an associate about a coil and possibly schedule a service for Tuesday. The agent informed the user that live assistance was unavailable but documented the request for a callback on the next business day. The user had no further questions and ended the call.

Derrick called after hours to report that he is still waiting for a service technician and missed a call from Nicole. The agent informed him that live transfers are not available after 4 PM but assured him that a representative will follow up on the next business day. The call ended with the agent confirming that all details were documented for follow-up.

The user called after hours requesting to speak with someone in the office, but the agent informed them that live assistance was not available and attempted to gather information for a follow-up. The call ended abruptly as the user hung up before providing details.

The user, Sage, called after hours seeking to connect with a customer service representative to continue a previous conversation and follow up on an item that was supposed to be sent by the end of the day. The agent informed Sage that live transfers were not available after hours but documented the request for a representative to follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further assistance requested.
The user requested to speak with an assistant, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the estimated time of arrival for a technician. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to inquire about service availability in their ZIP code 33166, but the agent informed them that service is not offered in that area. The user then requested to speak with Diego Constable, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative regarding a quote they received. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to reschedule an appointment to Thursday, July 16th, anytime after noon, as they are unavailable on Wednesday afternoon. The agent confirmed the new appointment details and assured a follow-up from a human agent to confirm availability. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.
The user called and requested to speak to someone in the office. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate agent.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully within a short call duration.

The user called to set up a water heater service appointment required by St. Louis County. The agent successfully booked the appointment for the user on Monday at 12 PM. However, the user expressed refusal to pay the dispatch fee and requested to speak with customer service, leading to a call transfer.
The call was very brief, with the agent initiating the conversation by asking for the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was successful.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call successfully to another agent.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation before the transfer.

The user, Marjorie Lewis, called to request air conditioner service at her address in Illinois, providing her contact details and a backup phone number for her daughter. The agent confirmed all information, including a correction to the email address, and assured that a human agent would follow up soon to confirm availability. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

The user called asking for Amy but was informed she was unavailable. The user mentioned someone had been at their door but was no longer there. The agent apologized for the inconvenience and transferred the call to someone who could provide live updates.

The user called to report a lockout at the address 1010153 Delridge after no one answered the door or phone. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Delsie Boyd, called to request a warranty repair for an air conditioning unit with a leak and no air. The agent booked a service appointment for the next day at 10 AM and explained that the exact cost would be reviewed by the team during the visit. The user provided feedback indicating some dissatisfaction with the lack of pricing information and desire to speak to a person.

The user, Shanika, called to schedule a water heater inspection that had not been previously inspected. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for July 20th at noon. The user was satisfied with the prompt service and provided positive feedback.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the user started to speak but did not complete their statement.
The user called Budget but quickly realized they had the wrong number and ended the call shortly after. The agent politely offered assistance if needed in the future.

The user called to return a missed call regarding Medicare and a refund but did not have the number. The agent informed the user that billing information was not accessible and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user called to schedule a missed water heater inspection after installation. The agent provided available Saturday time slots, and the user agreed to any of the offered times on July 18th. The call ended after confirming the appointment details without any issues.

The user called to confirm that their intake form was received and to schedule an air conditioning service. The agent acknowledged the request, documented it for follow-up, and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user requested a callback regarding the intake form and scheduling.

The user called to follow up on a service call related to their air conditioner but expressed frustration and requested to cancel the follow-up visit. The agent acknowledged the cancellation request and attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further customer service assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user called Budget and confirmed they were calling about a service request. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user, Ekalene from American Home Shield, called to provide upgrade information for a member regarding an air conditioner upgrade. The agent confirmed details, collected contact information, and agreed to have the team contact the member with all upgrade details. The user was satisfied and provided positive feedback before ending the call.

The user, Steven Stern, contacted Budget to schedule an urgent plumbing appointment for leaks at the main water line and washing machine. The agent successfully booked a service slot for the same afternoon at 4 PM and confirmed the details with the user. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback but expressing gratitude.
The user, Angela, called in referencing a previous call she received. The agent, Sophie, attempted to assist but transferred the call to another number for further help.

Tracy Campbell called to report a leaking upstairs toilet that has caused water damage and needs urgent plumbing service in the afternoons. The agent attempted to transfer her to a live emergency specialist but was unsuccessful, so the issue was escalated for a callback. Tracy requested to be contacted via text instead of a phone call due to being at the DMV.

Jamie White called to check if someone was on their way to their house after missing a call. The agent confirmed Jamie's identity and phone number but could not find any appointment under the provided information. Jamie declined to leave a message or be transferred to a live representative.
